summary: End-to-end test of the Decision Intelligence Engine MVP. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests passed covering health, ontology, CRUD, deterministic scoring, blocker logic, demo seed, and LLM report generation. Frontend: Full create→intake→evidence→report flow verified; demo briefing renders score 72; new assessment with all-approved evidence renders score 75 with Claude-generated narrative (942 chars, 4 strengths, 4 risks, 5 remediation actions); Export PDF button visible; methodology accordion functional.
critical_code_review_comments: Intake.jsx onBlurSave uses `initiative` from closure — concurrent blurs (e.g., tab-blur then stage-click) may persist a slightly stale snapshot. UI is unaffected (state is single source), but server-side autosave can briefly trail local state. Consider passing latest values explicitly or using a ref to avoid stale-closure writes.
server.py seed-demo contains a noop placeholder line `await db.reports.delete_many({"assessment_id": {"$in": []}})` — harmless but dead code; remove for clarity.

Report.jsx auto-falls back to POST /report if GET returns 404 — this is convenient but means simply landing on /report/:id of a fresh assessment will silently trigger an LLM call. Consider gating behind an explicit user action to avoid surprise spend.

No global error toasts/state in Landing/Intake/Assessment when API calls throw (catch blocks only console.error). User sees no feedback if backend is unreachable. Consider adding a minimal error state.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (core flows: landing, intake, evidence, score refresh, generate report, demo briefing, methodology toggle, export PDF)

seed_data_creation: Backend test suite creates several TEST_-prefixed assessments via API. Demo assessment seeded via POST /api/assessments/seed-demo; verified score=72, band=Structured, tier=Proceed to Constrained Pilot, no triggered blockers.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_die_backend.py — run with `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_die_backend.py -v`. Tests cover health, ontology, CRUD, scoring (empty + demo), blocker downgrade logic (no_technical_owner), demo seed, demo/current, full LLM report generation, and GET-report 404. Report-generation tests call Claude Sonnet 4.5 in real time — keep LLM_TIMEOUT at 90s. Frontend public URL: https://emergence-plan.preview.emergentagent.com. data-testid attributes are present on all interactive elements (begin-assessment-btn, view-demo-btn, intake-name/business_unit/description/target_workflow/expected_outcomes, intake-stage-{discovery|pilot|production_candidate}, intake-continue-btn, dimension-nav-{id}, maturity-{indicator_id}-{level}, next-dim-btn, generate-report-btn, score-hero-numeral, reasoning-narrative, strengths-list, risks-list, remediation-list, export-pdf-btn, methodology-toggle).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. An initial Playwright run reported 'Continue button still disabled' after filling intake fields — root cause was test-script timing (clicking the same input after fill caused focus oscillation that interfered with subsequent state). When the script properly blurred the name field by clicking the next input, all fields persisted in React state and the Continue button enabled correctly. Not a product bug.
